subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics from independent sources (IMF, World Bank, V-Dem, Freedom House, RSF, TI, ICG) + international press; Gabonese state media EXCLUDED there.

The Aug 2023 coup ended 55 years of Bongo rule; Gen. Oligui Nguema won the April 2025 election (~90%) under a new 2024 constitution - a transition with genuine reform signals but clear personalist-continuity risk.

Standout features: a rare post-coup press-freedom rebound and anti-corruption drive, against a high-debt (~70% GDP, junk-rated) declining-oil rentier economy, extreme inequality, and an exceptional carbon-sink forest. CFA euro-peg.

economic.notes: A peg-anchored, low-inflation but high-debt declining-oil rentier with a classic resource-curse profile.

fiscal_state.notes: Public debt ~70.5% of GDP (above the CEMAC ceiling) with arrears accumulation and a junk rating; declining oil weighs on the long path. (IMF Art IV; Fitch; World Bank)

monetary_independence.notes: No own policy, but the CEMAC CFA euro peg with a French convertibility guarantee delivers credible stability. (BEAC; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The CFA franc is regional, not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and peg-anchored near the CEMAC norm. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: A COBAC-supervised CEMAC system - shallow and low-penetration but peg-backed. (BEAC; IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: ~97% of exports are oil, manganese, and wood with structurally declining oil - a classic resource curse with modest diversification. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: Weak institutions with a genuine but unconsolidated post-coup reform trajectory.

rule_of_law.notes: Weak rule of law and entrenched patronage (CPI ~27/100), but a post-coup anti-corruption drive (Bongo aides jailed) and a new constitution offer a modest, selective improvement. (TI 2024; Freedom House 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Weak capacity but IMF-engaged and validated on macro data. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Patronage-laden and thin, though resource rents fund more capacity than poorer peers. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: A genuine post-coup rebound (RSF ~41st in 2025, 'satisfactory') but reforms unconsolidated and reversible. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: A coup-then-election transition with reform signals but continuity risk, over deep inequality.

political_stability.notes: A coup-then-election transition with credible turnout but a ~90% margin and incumbency advantage risking a new personalist settlement. (Al Jazeera; France24)

civil_liberties.notes: Improved tone post-coup but still constrained - a 'Partly Free' trajectory. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A significant West African migrant-labour presence; not rights-protective but no mass-expulsion pattern documented. (inference)

social_cohesion.notes: Extreme inequality (~35% poverty) despite high per-capita wealth; no active insurgency. (World Bank)

demographics.notes: Young and urbanised - a potential dividend against a 20%+ jobs strain. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A stable, France-linked, sanction-free actor with no active conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: France ties retained post-coup; pragmatic and non-confrontational, with no Russia-pivot drift seen. (ICG; Reuters)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active armed conflict; stable borders. (ACLED)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Post-coup AU/ECCAS suspensions lifted after the transition; operates within CEMAC FX. (BEAC; Reuters)

physical_practical.notes: A rare carbon sink with low climate hazard, against weak healthcare and uneven infrastructure.

climate.notes: ~88% forest cover buffers and low population pressure, against coastal-flood exposure and oil-dependent readiness. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: Weak but oil-funded above the poorest peers, with an e-health push underway. (World Bank; WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Better than the poorest CEMAC peers (Libreville, manganese rail) but uneven power and roads. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Urban crime in Libreville/Port-Gentil; no insurgency. (OSAC)

summary: The regional outlier on trajectory: the August 2023 coup ended 55 years of Bongo rule, and Gen. Oligui Nguema legitimised himself in an April 2025 election (~90%, ~70% turnout) under a new 2024 constitution - a transition with genuine reform signals (a striking RSF press-freedom rebound, jailed Bongo-era officials) but clear personalist-continuity risk in the lopsided margin. The economy is a high-debt (~70.5% of GDP, junk-rated), declining-oil rentier with relatively high but deeply unequal per-capita wealth, partly offset by manganese, timber, and an exceptional carbon-sink forest. The CFA euro peg anchors monetary stability and low inflation, leaving a symmetric skew: the upside of a real institutional opening against the downside of a reformist-strongman reversion.
